lede: A look at April 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mirra Andreeva and Sara Errani, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Baltimore Orioles
events.desc: A baseball game between the Baltimore Orioles and the Chicago White Sox sets the all-time low attendance mark for Major League Baseball. Zero fans were in attendance for the game, as the stadium was officially closed to the public due to the 2015 Baltimore protests.

events.title: Chemical Weapons Convention
events.desc: The Chemical Weapons Convention of 1993 enters into force, outlawing the production, stockpiling and use of chemical weapons by its signatories.

events.title: 1991 Bangladesh cyclone
events.desc: A cyclone strikes the Chittagong district of southeastern Bangladesh with winds of around 155 miles per hour (249 km/h), killing at least 138,000 people and leaving as many as ten million homeless.

events.title: Sikhs
events.desc: An assembly of Sikhs, known as a Sarbat Khalsa, officially declare independence for a state of Khalistan.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: United States and South Vietnamese forces invade Cambodia to interdict the Ho Chi Minh Trail in an attempt to cut off supplies to the Viet Cong and the North Vietnamese Army.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Surrender of Caserta is signed by the commander of German forces in Italy.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: World War II: Adolf Hitler marries his longtime partner Eva Braun in a Berlin bunker and designates Admiral Karl Dönitz as his successor.

events.title: Parliament of the United Kingdom
events.desc: The Parliament of the United Kingdom passes the People's Budget, the first budget in British history with the expressed intent of redistributing wealth among the British public.

events.title: Frank Slide
events.desc: A landslide kills 70 people in Frank, in the District of Alberta, Canada.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British and French ships clash in the Battle of Fort Royal off the coast of Martinique.

deaths.text: Dan Walker (politician), American lawyer and politician, 36th Governor of Illinois
Avigdor Arikha, French-Israeli artist, printmaker and art historian
Michael Gordon (film director), American actor and director

observances.text: Christian feast day: Catherine of Siena (Catholic, Lutheran and Anglican Church)
Shōwa Day, traditionally the start of the Golden Week holiday period, which is April 29 and May 3–5. (Japan)
